title: Share your thoughts: Proposed Code of Practice for the Environmentally Sound Management of Chemical Substances in the Chemicals, Plastics and Rubber Sectors - Canada.ca
content:
## Share your thoughts: Proposed Code of Practice for the Environmentally Sound Management of Chemical Substances in the Chemicals, Plastics and Rubber Sectors
From: Environment and Climate Change Canada

## Current status: Closed
The Government of Canada is soliciting your feedback on the proposed Code of Practice for the Environmentally Sound Management of Chemical Substances in the Chemicals, Plastics and Rubber Sectors . It is open for a 60-day public comment period ending on April 5, 2023.
The input gathered through this process will be taken into consideration when preparing the final code of practice.
The purpose of the proposed code is to provide guidance on best practices to manufacturers, formulators and distributors in the chemicals, plastics and rubber sectors in order to prevent releases of chemical substances to the environment. Releases of chemical substances to the environment can originate from a variety of sources and activities within these sectors.
In addition to providing general guidance to all facilities in the targeted sectors, Environment and Climate Change Canada intends to use the proposed code as a complementary risk management instrument for some substances assessed to be toxic under the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 .

## Join in: how to participate
You are invited to review and submit your comments on on the proposed code of practice for the environmentally sound management of chemical substances in the chemicals, plastics and rubber sectors.
Send an email with your ideas or comments using the contact information below.
Send a letter with your ideas or comments to the address in the contact information below.
Please include “Consultation on Code of Practice for the Environmentally Sound Management of Chemicals” in the subject line of your email or letter.
Any person who provides information to the Minister of the Environment may request that it be treated as confidential under section 313 of the Canadian Environmental Protection Act, 1999 .
Please read our privacy statement for this consultation.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We would like to hear from:
- manufacturers, formulators and distributors in the chemicals, plastics and rubber sectors
- non-governmental organizations
- Indigenous communities
- other interested persons

## Key documents for discussion
- Proposed Code of Practice for the Environmentally Sound Management of Chemical Substances in the Chemicals, Plastics and Rubber Sectors
- List of environmental codes of practice - Canada.ca
Email: pgpc-dppc-cmp-cpd@ec.gc.ca
Plastics and Chemicals Unit Chemical Production Division 351 St. Joseph Blvd Gatineau QC  K1A 0H3

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
